weatherForecast.comment: During the night, the clouds increase from the south and the wind dies down. On Wednesday, the mountains on the southern side of the Alps will be covered in fog. In the morning it will start to rain and snow along the Gurktal Alps, the southern Niedere Tauern and the Seetal Alps. The snowfall level fluctuates between 1000 and 1500 metres above sea level. The heaviest snowfall will be on Thursday night. The wind will pick up again during the day. In the afternoon, moderate to strong winds with gusts between 40 and 60 km/h from the south. The temperature at an altitude of 2000 metres will be around -2 degrees. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres will be between -1 and +1 degree. Amounts of new snow between a few centimetres in the northern Niedere Tauern and up to 10 cm between the Turracher Höhe and the Zirbitzkogel are expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Persistent weak layer problem! Take particular care in gullies and bowls in northern and eastern exposures!
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is assessed as moderate above the tree line and low below. There is still a persistent weak layer problem in the northern and eastern exposures. In addition, fresh pillows of wind drifted snow could form in the same aspects, which can be triggered by low additional loads. Medium-sized slab avalanches are possible when breaking through the weak old snowpack. The danger areas are mainly located in the transition from little to more snow as well as behind terrain edges and in the entrance area of gullies and bowls. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes difficult to recognise due to a few centimetres of new fallen snow.

snowpackStructure.comment: The total snow depth is well below average. With the southerly wind, thin pillows of wind drifted snow have formed in northern aspects. These cover a very variable and mostly hardness old snowpack. There is significantly more snow in shady gullies and bowls than on sunny slopes, some of which are partly bare up to 2000 metres. In the northern and eastern exposures, the snowpack is still weakened by layers with faceted crystals and floating snow (persistent weak layer problem!).

tendency.highlights: An Italian low will bring snowfall and rain on Wednesday. The snowfall level will be between 800 and 1500 metres above sea level. There will be hardly any precipitation on the northern side of the Alps. The southerly wind will ease in the west. The avalanche risk increases slightly with the new fallen snow.

weatherForecast.comment: In the northern Alps and generally at altitudes above 1500 metres, the night will be cloudless. Clouds will gather from the south on Tuesday. While the peaks of the western Northern Alps will remain fog-free, visibility will deteriorate from Hochschwab to Veitsch. However, there will be no precipitation. The temperature at an altitude of 2000 metres will be between -8 and -1 degrees. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres will be between -4 and 2 degrees. Brisk to stormy winds with gusts between 40 and 60 km/h from south to south-west.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger, but persistent weak layer problems in the northern and eastern exposures!
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. In the northern and eastern exposures, there is still a persistent weak layer problem at altitudes above the tree line. In addition, fresh pillows of wind drifted snow have formed with the strong south-westerly wind. In steep terrain, these can be triggered in a few places. However, even if the old snowpack is torn through, the avalanches usually remain small. In general, the risk of entrainment, falling and injury outweighs the risk of burial. However, be careful in the area of terrain traps.

snowpackStructure.comment: There is still significantly less snow than average. A thin, partly bound snowpack covers a very variable and mostly hard old snowpack. There is significantly more snow in shady gullies and bowls than on sunny slopes, some of which are partly bare up to 2000 metres. In the northern and eastern exposures, the snowpack is still weakened by layers with faceted crystals and floating snow (persistent weak layer problem!).

tendency.highlights:
The avalanche risk is not expected to increase.
In the night to Thursday, some new fallen snow will only fall in the eastern northern Alps, in the west it will remain mostly precipitation-free. During the day there will still be some clouds and fog in windless conditions. Apart from a few snowflakes, no significant new fallen snow is expected.

weatherForecast.comment: During the night, the clouds increase from the south and in the morning it starts to rain and snow along the edge of the mountains. The wind dies down overnight. On Wednesday, the mountains of the Randgebirge are covered in fog and it snows a little. The snowfall level fluctuates between 1000 and 1500 metres above sea level. The heaviest snowfall will be on Thursday night. The wind will pick up again during the day. In the afternoon, moderate to strong winds with gusts between 40 and 60 km/h from south to west. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres will be around -1 degrees. The temperature at an altitude of 1000 metres will be around +1 degree. Amounts of new snow between a few centimetres in the eastern foothills and up to 10 cm on the Koralm are expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger with mostly little snow.
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. In extremely steep gullies or bowls on shady slopes, the triggering of small slab avalanches cannot be completely ruled out.

snowpackStructure.comment: There is still significantly less snow than average. A thin, partly bound snowpack covers a very variable and mostly hard old snowpack. In shady gullies and bowls, the connection between the bound snowpack and the old snowpack is sometimes poor. In addition, angular shapes can weaken the snow base, fundament.

tendency.highlights:
The risk of avalanches will increase slightly with the snowfall on Thursday night.
During the day, there will still be some clouds and fog in windless conditions. Apart from a few snowflakes, no significant new fallen snow is expected.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Note the persistent weak layer problem in the northern and eastern exposures!
